name: City tour por Bilbao
region_intro: Jogging around Bilbao offers a diverse landscape for runners, blending urban routes with accessible natural areas. The city is characterized by its central Estuary, which provides extensive waterfront promenades and parks. Beyond the urban core, the Anillo Verde (Green Ring) connects various city and forest parks, offering panoramic views. Surrounding natural parks like Urkiola and Gorbeia feature dramatic limestone peaks, dense forests, and varied terrain for more challenging trail running. Best jogging routes around Bilbao The most popular jogging route is City tour por Bilbao, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 31 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a scenic urban experience through the city. Another top favourite among local runners is Zubizuri Bridge – Pedro Arrupe Pedestrian Bridge loop from Zabalburu, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This route follows the Estuary, offering views of iconic city bridges. Local runners also love the Zubizuri Bridge – The Sirgueras loop from San Ignazio, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail leading through the Estuary area, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Jogging around Bilbao is defined by urban riverfront paths, extensive green rings, and surrounding natural parks with forests and hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy city runs to more challenging trail running. The routes in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 8000 runners have used komoot to explore Bilbao's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Bilbao?
A: Bilbao offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 100 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy city runs to more challenging trail running adventures in the surrounding natural parks.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed pace?
A: Yes, Bilbao has several easy jogging routes. For a pleasant urban experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059032905/city-tour-por-bilbao" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour por Bilbao&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) trail that takes about 31 minutes to complete. Many of the city's parks also offer gentle paths.

Q: What are the best areas for scenic running with city views?
A: For impressive panoramic views of Bilbao, routes around the &lt;strong&gt;Anillo Verde (Green Ring)&lt;/strong&gt;, particularly near Monte Avril, Pagasarri, and Artxanda, are highly recommended. The Estuary also offers stunning city skyline views, especially along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24744687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubizuri Bridge – Pedro Arrupe Pedestrian Bridge loop from Zabalburu&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running paths that are family-friendly in Bilbao?
A: Absolutely. Urban parks like &lt;strong&gt;Europa Parkea&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Doña Casilda Iturrizar Park&lt;/strong&gt; offer pleasant, well-maintained paths suitable for families. These parks often have amenities and diverse scenery, making them ideal for a relaxed run with children.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Bilbao?
A: Many of Bilbao's green spaces and natural areas are dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept on a leash. The extensive paths along the Estuary and within the larger urban parks like Europa Parkea are popular choices for runners with their canine companions. Always check local regulations for specific park rules.

Q: What kind of landmarks or attractions can I see while running in Bilbao?
A: Bilbao's running routes often pass by significant landmarks. Along the Estuary, you can admire the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/335425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guggenheim Museum&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2911339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubizuri Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4628863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;'Puppy' sculpture&lt;/a&gt;. Parks like Doña Casilda Iturrizar Park also offer beautiful scenery and historical elements.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Bilbao are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24744687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubizuri Bridge – Pedro Arrupe Pedestrian Bridge loop from Zabalburu&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 5.3-mile (8.5 km) circular path along the Estuary. The &lt;strong&gt;Anillo Verde&lt;/strong&gt; also provides numerous circular options of varying lengths and difficulties.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Bilbao?
A: The running routes in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse terrain, from urban riverfront paths to extensive green rings and challenging natural park trails, offering options for all ability levels.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging trail runs near Bilbao?
A: For those seeking more challenging trail runs, the wider Bizkaia region offers stunning natural parks. &lt;strong&gt;Urkiola Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, about 40 minutes from Bilbao, features dramatic limestone peaks and birch forests, while &lt;strong&gt;Gorbeia Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; provides extensive trails through beech, oak, and pine forests. Some sections of the &lt;strong&gt;Anillo Verde&lt;/strong&gt; also extend over 70 km, offering significant challenges.

Q: Can I access running routes using public transport in Bilbao?
A: Yes, Bilbao's excellent public transport network makes many running routes easily accessible. The Estuary paths, for example, can be reached via subway, allowing for one-way runs. Many urban parks are also well-connected by bus or metro, making it convenient to start and finish your run.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by lakes or water features?
A: Beyond the Estuary, you can find routes that incorporate lakes and other water features. &lt;strong&gt;Doña Casilda Iturrizar Park&lt;/strong&gt; features a charming duck pond. For a more natural setting, the &lt;strong&gt;Lakes of the Old Iron Mines&lt;/strong&gt; or the &lt;strong&gt;Gorostiza Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt; offer scenic running opportunities, often with dedicated trails.

Q: What is the best season for running in Bilbao?
A: Bilbao generally has a mild climate, making it suitable for running year-round. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be warm but are often tempered by coastal breezes, while winters are mild, though rain is more common.
tips: Also called the “Calatrava Bridge” after the architect who built it, it is an arched, white and slender structure that looks like a sailing ship ten meters high above the river mouth. Its glass surface has caused a lot of controversy, which is why today people step on a non-slip mat. It connects Uribarte with Campo Volantín and, despite the controversies that have always accompanied it, is one of the symbols of the city.

Large spider-shaped sculpture by the French-American artist Louise Bourgeois (1911-2010). It is made of bronze, stainless steel and marble. It weighs 22 tons, and measures 10 meters in height and 10 in diameter.
The Guggenheim museum not only cleaned the face of the dirty Bilbao of the 80's but also gave the city a modern and avant-garde air. A 'must' to visit in this city.

The Iberdroler Tower is a 165 m high skyscraper. It was designed by César Pelli and officially inaugurated on 21.2.2012 by King Juan Carlos I. It is the ninth largest in all of Spain and also the tallest in northern Spain.
